input.background: Suppose that asthma affects 1 in 20 children in a population. You take an SRS of 50 children from this population.
input.question: Can the Normal approximation to the binomial be applied under these conditions?

output.method: 事件发生概率及独立性
output.answer: $n p q=(50)(0.05)(0.95)=2.375$. Therefore, the sample is too small to apply the Normal approximation.

asthma_prevalence.description: The prevalence of asthma in the population, which is 1 in 20 children.

sample_size.description: The size of the simple random sample (SRS) taken from the population.

normal_approximation_applicability.description: Whether the Normal approximation to the binomial distribution can be applied under the given conditions.

meta_info.caption: Pediatric asthma survey, n = 50.
meta_info.section: 8.3 SAMPLING BEHAVIOR OF A COUNT AND PROPORTION
